mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0% and remains elevated for two consecutive months, equity risk premiums expand due to higher discount rates and reduced corporate profit margins. This triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days as investors reprice growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:current FEDFUNDS at 5.33% indicates restrictive monetary stance
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with rates above neutral
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 5.0% for 2+ months, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained payroll employment growth above 150,000 persons per month signals robust labor market conditions that support consumer spending and corporate earnings. This always-on mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the labor market strength translates into revenue growth.
evidence_refs: fact:current PAYEMS at 156,923 thousand indicates strong employment level
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of months when PAYEMS > 150,000, or correlation between PAYEMS growth rate and 60-day forward returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales (RSAFS) decline by more than 2% month-over-month while simultaneously housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1,200 thousand units, aggregate demand deteriorates sharply. This joint contraction signals recession risk and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as forward guidance revisions cascade through equity markets.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 2% MoM AND HOUST < 1,200 thousand units
evidence_refs: fact:current RSAFS at 704,954 million and HOUST at 1,358 thousand
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the joint condition fails to predict negative returns with directional accuracy > 55% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month while the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 4.0%, signaling simultaneous supply weakness and labor market softening. This regime-specific interaction predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as stagflation concerns emerge and equity valuations compress.
evidence_refs: fact:current INDPRO at 103.6115 and UNRATE at 3.9%
regime_hint: stagflation or late-cycle slowdown with labor market deterioration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between the interaction signal and forward returns falls below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises above 310 index points (approximately 3.5% YoY equivalent) and the federal funds rate remains below 5.5%, real rates turn negative, eroding equity risk premiums. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as investors rotate into equities as inflation hedges.
evidence_refs: fact:current CPIAUCSL at 307.619 and FEDFUNDS at 5.33%
regime_hint: negative real rates with persistent inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the mechanism fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy with statistical significance (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarter-over-quarter decelerates below 0.5% annualized rate, indicating late-cycle slowdown. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as earnings growth expectations are revised downward and equity multiples compress.
evidence_refs: fact:current GDPC1 at 22,491.567 billion chained 2017 dollars
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of quarters when GDPC1 growth < 0.5%, or Rank IC between GDPC1 growth and forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month rolling window, signaling deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer confidence. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as forward earnings estimates are cut due to expected demand weakness.
evidence_refs: fact:current UNRATE at 3.9% indicates tight labor market
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early recession signal
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the directional accuracy of the signal falls below 52% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) remain above 1,300 thousand units on a 3-month moving average, indicating sustained residential investment and construction employment. This always-on mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as housing strength supports consumer wealth effects and multiplier-driven GDP growth.
evidence_refs: fact:current HOUST at 1,358 thousand units indicates robust housing activity
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of months when HOUST > 1,300 thousand, or correlation between HOUST levels and 60-day forward returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.10).
